title: Unfortunately Yours
author: Tessa Bailey
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Unfortunately Yours" book by Tessa Bailey?
Options:
A. Hallie cheered loudly and raised a glass of wine in celebration. I agree completely. Now you go, August, Julian told him. Was Julian grinning mockingly? August didn't even feel the axe when he took hold of it. He flipped it around a few times, looked at it, and realized it was trembling. Shoot. Uh, would someone else like to take a turn? We have to go in order, Julian said, gesturing at the rules. With no choice, August ensured nobody was too close, then flung the axe - observing queasily as it landed near the outer ring. Nobody said anything when he moved back and motioned for Natalie to take her turn.
B. Hallie whooped in celebration and lifted a glass of wine. You got that right. It's your turn now, August, Julian said to him. Was Julian smirking in amusement? August didn't notice the weight of the axe when he picked it up. He turned it over several times, looked at it, and saw it shaking. Yikes. Uh, would someone else like a turn? We must take turns in order, Julian reminded them, pointing to the rules. Having no way out, August made sure no one was too close by, then hurled the axe - watching uncomfortably as it landed near the outer edge. No one commented when he stepped back and indicated for Natalie to take her turn.
C. Hallie whooped and held up a glass of wine. I know thats right. Your turn, August, Julian prompted. Was he smirking? August couldnt even feel the axe in his hand when he picked it up. He turned it over a few times, looked down, and found it shaking. Damn. Uh, does someone else want to take a turn? Turns must go in order, Julian said, pointing at the rule sheet. Having no choice, August made sure no one was standing too close, then threw the weaponwatching with a sour stomach as it landed in the outer ring. No one said anything when he stepped back and gestured for Natalie to take her turn.
D. Hallie cheered and raised a glass of wine. I agree with that. Now it's your go, August, Julian told him. Was Julian grinning slyly? August couldn't even sense the axe when he grabbed it. He flipped it around a few times, looked down, and saw it trembling. Shoot. Uh, can someone else have a turn? Turns must follow order, Julian said, indicating the rules. With no option, August ensured no one was too near, then tossed the tool - watching queasily as it landed in the outer circle. Nobody spoke when he moved back and motioned for Natalie to take her turn.
Answer:
